headline: United Airlines Beats Earnings Estimates but Lowers Guidance Due to Oil Prices
summary: For the full year, United changed its profit guidance range to $7-$11 a share, compared with its previous earnings guidance of $12 to $14 a share.

headline: Every Wall Street Analyst Says Buy Alaska Air. The Stock Has Lost 43% in a Decade.
summary: Alaska Air Group (NYSE: ALK) carries a unanimous Wall Street buy consensus: 4 Strong Buy and 12 Buy ratings, zero Holds, zero Sells, across 16 analysts covering the stock. The stock has delivered a −43.8% return over the past decade. That gap between what analysts say and what shareholders have experienced is the central question ... headline: United Airlines Earnings Land Next. Merger Talk, Fuel Costs, Airfares, and More to Watch.
summary: United Airlines  investors saw one flight path to growth closed off as  American Airlines  categorically snubbed its merger pitch last week.  The stock has been in recovery mode, up more than 7% this month but subject to the whims of volatile oil markets and elevated jet fuel prices brought on by the Iran war.  United shares are down about 12% in 2026, lagging behind rival  Delta Air Lines  up 2.6%, and  Southwest Airlines  up 1.2%.

headline: After Fifteen Years, Tim Cook Steps Down, Wall Street Steps Back, Oil Steps Up
summary: When the session ended Monday and the Iran headlines had finally cycled through their daily contradictions, Apple dropped the one piece of news that nobody had circled on their calendar: Tim Cook is stepping down as CEO.

headline: Delta Eases Climate Targets As Fuel Challenges Reshape Long Term Risks
summary: Delta Air Lines has removed its 10% sustainable aviation fuel target for 2030 from its sustainability page. The airline has also softened its net zero emissions language, reflecting jet fuel supply challenges. These changes adjust a key part of Delta’s climate strategy and may influence how investors view long term risks. For investors watching NYSE:DAL, these revisions come as the stock trades at $71.21, with a value score of 4 and a 1 year return of 82.6%. Over the past 3 years the stock...

headline: Delta Air Lines (DAL) Is Up 6.2% After Fuel-Driven Route Cuts and Q2 Guidance Update – Has The Bull Case Changed?
summary: Earlier in April 2026, Delta Air Lines reported first-quarter revenue of US$15,854 million but moved from net income of US$240 million a year earlier to a net loss of US$289 million, while issuing second-quarter guidance for low-teens year-over-year revenue growth, a 6%–8% operating margin, and earnings per share of US$1.00–US$1.50. At the same time, Delta’s jet fuel costs roughly doubled and the airline began trimming routes to focus on more profitable flying, highlighting how cost...
